TEXT.source: NTU Corpus stories for Kavalan, story: KavCon-relatives_buya_ngengi

S.FORM: tangi buya mawtu isu tapawanku.
S.TRANSL: 今天, Buya, 你來我家。
Today, Buya, you came to my home.

S.FORM: nengi ti mlizaq aiku.
S.TRANSL: 很好,我很高興。
(It's) good. I am happy.

S.FORM: qawtuansu mlawut ya ay aiku ya qaqasu haw.
S.TRANSL: 你來拜訪我,你的姊姊。
You came to visit me, your sister.

S.FORM: mai iku tu nianu saiza buya.
S.TRANSL: 我沒有什麼。
I have nothing.

S.FORM: nengi ti angtangisi qan pa ita saRabi saiza patawsi pa ita zinku ya.
S.TRANSL: 沒關係.等會,我們一起吃晚餐,我們喝酒,我說
It doesn’t matter. Later, we will eat dinner and we will drink, said I.

S.FORM: kwa ya.
S.TRANSL: 好的。
Okay.

S.FORM: tengnes ti iku mai mawtu msiqaz iku qaya tu.
S.TRANSL: 我很久沒來,我也不好意思。
I haven’t come for a long time. I feel sorry too.

S.FORM: mzaqi ita ma tengnes ma qawqa mawtu qausiq.
S.TRANSL: 我們(住的)近, (但)我很久才來一次。
We (lived) nearby, (but) I come here once in a while.

S.FORM: mai ita mlawut sukaw ti tu qaqaqa haw en.
S.TRANSL: 我們沒去拜訪, 對姊姊們不好。
We do not visit (them), (and) it is bad for sisters.

S.FORM: nayau saizi.
S.TRANSL: 這樣也是。
That’s right.

S.FORM: m aita qenaswani iza haw qena qenaswani azu titiana qa qawman yau ti aiku haw tanian qenaswani wi lawutanku.
S.TRANSL: 親友不管在哪裡,很遠我也去拜訪。
No matter where the relatives live, I would visit them even it’s far.

S.FORM: masang taqalinquan saiza.
S.TRANSL: 以前在花蓮。
In the past, in Hualien.

S.FORM: matiw iku sa taipak saiza.
S.TRANSL: 我去台北。
I went to Taipei.

S.FORM: sa jilung matiw ita tmita tu qenaswanita zaka isu.
S.TRANSL: 我們去看親戚,我這樣跟你講。
We went to see our relatives, I told you.

S.FORM: yau qabaqi sibaqiansu dalu titipil.
S.TRANSL: 還有你的長輩,Tipil。
There is you elder, Tipil.

S.FORM: lawutika qaya m.
S.TRANSL: 你去拜訪他。
Visit him.

S.FORM: matiw isu yau ti bawutna yau Raqna.
S.TRANSL: 你去的話,他有魚他有酒。
(If) you go, he has fish and he has wine.

S.FORM: yau saizi.
S.TRANSL: 是有啊。
There is.

S.FORM: sapatawsianna timaisu zaka.
S.TRANSL: 是他要請你喝酒的。
(these) are to treat you to drinking wine.

S.FORM: qmRas ti timaikuan.
S.TRANSL: 他也會叫我。
He will call me.

S.FORM: qatiwka zinna iku nani.
S.TRANSL: 他叫我去。
‘Go,’ he said to me.

S.FORM: en mana mai ita tu lawal ka.
S.TRANSL: 我們沒有空閒。
We do not have free time.

S.FORM: azu ti saRayRayngu ti tu baqita tayan ti tipilan.
S.TRANSL: 好像我們裝做不知道我們的長輩, Tipil。
It seems as if we did not know our elder, Tipil.

S.FORM: m siRap qatiwanku qaya qausiq nani.
S.TRANSL: 之前,我去過一次。
Not long ago, I went (there) once.

S.FORM: yau talazanan.
S.TRANSL: 有在路邊。
By the road

S.FORM: sapatawsianna timaisu zaka.
S.TRANSL: 是他要請你喝酒的。
(these) are to treat you to drinking wine.

S.FORM: qmRas ti timaikuan.
S.TRANSL: 他也會叫我。
He will call me.

S.FORM: qatiwka zinna iku nani.
S.TRANSL: 他叫我去。
‘Go,’ he said to me.

S.FORM: en mana mai ita tu lawal ka.
S.TRANSL: 我們沒有空閒。
We do not have free time.

S.FORM: azu ti saRayRayngu ti tu baqita tayan ti tipilan.
S.TRANSL: 好像我們裝做不知道我們的長輩, Tipil。
It seems as if we did not know our elder, Tipil.

S.FORM: m siRap qatiwanku qaya qausiq nani.
S.TRANSL: 之前,我去過一次。
Not long ago, I went (there) once.

S.FORM: yau talazanan.
S.TRANSL: 有在路邊。
By the road.

S.FORM: Hm yau RiRaq qaniyau talazanan nani.
S.TRANSL: 他們在路邊喝酒。
They drank wine by the road.

S.FORM: ya tuqazka zinna iku.
S.TRANSL: ‘進來’,他們對我說。
‘Come in,’ they said to me.

S.FORM: tmuqaz ti imi ya.
S.TRANSL: 我們就進去。
We went in.

S.FORM: yau ti imi tayan silalaq silalaq silalaq silalaq ti qaniyau ya atu kaputna nani.
S.TRANSL: 我們在哪裡跟他們還有他們的朋友聊天。
We were there chatting with them and their friends.

S.FORM: tipanay an nani azu yau lawal si qawtuka uman zinna iku nani.
S.TRANSL: Tipil跟我說,如果有時間的話,就再來喔。
Tipil said to me, “If (you) have time, come (to visit me) again.”

S.FORM: kwa zinku.
S.TRANSL: 我說好。
I said, “Okay.”

S.FORM: tengnes ti qaya mai matiw pilay ti ‘nay.
S.TRANSL: 我也很久沒去了, (跑那麼遠去)很累阿。
It’s been a long time since I went there last time as (going that far to visit him) is tiring.

S.FORM: taRaw m taRaw anemna sayza tu mana mai mawtu ‘nay sunis zau tmita timaikuan zinna ya.
S.TRANSL: 他也許會心痛, 想說為什麼這孩子不來看我呢?
Maybe that would hurt his heart, making him wonder why this child would not come to see me.

S.FORM: en.
S.TRANSL: 對。
Yes.

S.FORM: nayau zuma matiw iku ta takilung nani matiw iku talepawan na qaqa tiangaw.
S.TRANSL: 比方說我去基隆, 我去Angaw哥哥那邊的時候。
For example, (when) I go to Keelong, (that is), I go to brother Angaw’s place.

S.FORM: kaw mana wama aisu mawtu ay.
S.TRANSL: 為什麼來的只有你呢?
Why is it that the one who came was you only?

S.FORM: mana mai mawtu tiamuy qanibuya.
S.TRANSL: 為什麼Amuy和Buya他們那些人沒有來呢?
Why didn’t (those guys such as) Amuy and Buya come as well?’

S.FORM: tyatyana aita kebalan mai mautu mlawut timaikuan nazau.
S.TRANSL: 那些人不來拜訪我。
They don’t come to visit me.

S.FORM: Rayngu ti iku smaqay.
S.TRANSL: 我不會走路了。
I cannot walk.

S.FORM: pilay ti ma.
S.TRANSL: 只是有點累。
I’m a bit tired.

S.FORM: Rayngu iku Rayngu ti iku tu lazan en zinna en.
S.TRANSL: 我不知道路, 他這樣說。
I don’t know the way (to your palce), said he.

S.FORM: nayau ti quniakita sanuakati qaniyau nanlepaw tayan qawman qawtu ti mlawut zinku.
S.TRANSL: 我說 ‘好, 我會跟他們說, 叫他們一定要來拜訪。'
I said, ‘All right, I’ll tell them to come. They must come to visit (you).’

S.FORM: ta taRni iku tu mai sunis a yau mlawut tu naquni qawman matiw ita nani.
S.TRANSL: 我怎麼知道小孩如何拜訪的. 我們真的去的話。
How do I know how children go visiting. If we do go.

S.FORM: o mai imu mlawut qawman zinna timaita haw.
S.TRANSL: 他絕對會跟我們說, “你們怎麼都沒有來”
He will absolutely tell us, “Why didn’t you visit (me)?”

S.FORM: mai ita nayau.
S.TRANSL: 我們沒有(去), 就那樣。
We didn’t (go), and that’s it.

S.FORM: wantima ‘nay sabaqiananta tazian ‘nay zana Raqit qaqata zinku.
S.TRANSL: 我想我們在這邊的長輩只剩下他(Tipil)了。
We only have him as our elder here, I think.

S.FORM: en maqen ti ‘nay pataz tasaw saleqiaw ita tazian.
S.TRANSL: 是阿, 的確是這樣, 我們每年都在這裡跳舞。
Yes, we dance here every year.

S.FORM: bulaanku ti qaya tu saqawtuan tu iza tu.
S.TRANSL: 我也給他邀請卡。
I also gave him an invitation card.

S.FORM: sudad ‘nay en qawtuka zinku.
S.TRANSL: 邀請卡. 我叫他來。
Invitation card. I tell him to come.

S.FORM: nayau saiza sukaw ti zapanna Rayngu ti smaqay mai ti mawtu ya.
S.TRANSL: 他的腳有問題,不能走路不能來。
There are some problems with his legs. (He) cannot walk. (He) cannot come.

S.FORM: muRetut ti iku yau qaqa ti‘zun mawtu.
S.TRANSL: 我很驚訝’zun哥哥有來。
I was surprised that brother ‘zun came.

S.FORM: m taRaw zinna.
S.TRANSL: 別人說他生病了。
Others said that he was sick.

S.FORM: akwa mana yau qaqaku ti‘zun ka zinku.
S.TRANSL: 'zun哥哥怎麼會在呢?'我說。
‘How come my brother ‘zun was there?,’ said I.

S.FORM: nayau qenalizaqku tu qenawtu na baqbaq na qaqaku saiza zinku.
S.TRANSL: 我高興我哥哥來了。
I was glad that my brother came.

S.FORM: Ranaw kasianem iku titipilan masang.
S.TRANSL: 所以我想到tipil那邊去。
Thus, I would like to go to tipil’s place.

S.FORM: m en mana wantima aizipna mai mawtu zinku.
S.TRANSL: 為何只有他沒來?
How come only he didn’t come?

S.FORM: Ranaw sikawma zimaisu.
S.TRANSL: 所以我才跟你說。
Thus, I told you (this).

S.FORM: quniwanai yau qaitunsu saiza azasika mawtu sa sa sa lepawta sa taipakan qatabanta saiza.
S.TRANSL: 下次,你有車子的話,帶他到我們家到台北來我們的豐年祭。
(If) you have car, next time take him to our house, to Taipei (for) the harvest ritual.

S.FORM: zaka isu zinku.
S.TRANSL: 我這樣跟你說。
I told you so.

S.FORM: maqen ti that baqiniq ti‘zun siRap mawtu ay nani.
S.TRANSL: 我們的長輩’zun昨天來。
Our elder ‘zun came yesterday.

S.FORM: muRetut muRetut ti iku anemku muRetut anemku tmita tu baqi ‘nay.
S.TRANSL: 我很驚訝看到長輩。
I was surprised to see the elder.

S.FORM: tu baqita tengnes ti mai mqaita.
S.TRANSL: 我們的舅舅很久沒見到(我們)。
Our uncle hasn’t seen (us) for a long time.

S.FORM: tangi mlabu tu qenalisinbuta tazian.
S.TRANSL: 今天他參加我們這裡的聚會。
Today, he participated in our gathering here.

S.FORM: tu saleqiawanta zinku nani.
S.TRANSL: 我們在那裡跳舞,我說。
We danced there, said I.

S.FORM: azasanku ti kwa qawitanku ti pasazui ta.
S.TRANSL: 我帶著他,牽著他的手,到...
I took him, holding his hand, to...

S.FORM: tangayauan.
S.TRANSL: 在前面。
To the front.

S.FORM: tangayauan.
S.TRANSL: 在前面。
To the front.

S.FORM: paqalel tangayauan.
S.TRANSL: (我)請他坐到前面。
(I) asked him to sit in the front.

S.FORM: nayau ti qalizaqan tmita ti iku tu baq sunista babaqita swanita qawman zin na anemna.
S.TRANSL: 他在心裡應該這麼想, ‘我很高興看到孩子,我的晚輩’ 。
He probably said in his heart, ‘I am glad to see the children, my juniors.’

S.FORM: nayauanta ya wawaita na kabalan mai ita msukaw tu qenaswani.
S.TRANSL: 我們噶瑪蘭的行為應該這樣:不要忘了親友。
Our behavior of Kavalan should be like that: Don’t forget our relatives.

S.FORM: mai ita sukaw tu izau tu baqbaqita mniz.
S.TRANSL: 我們不能忘記全部的長輩。
We cannot forget our relatives.

S.FORM: tinu tinu qaya qalinku tu qenaswani tazian.
S.TRANSL: 誰會忘記這裡的親友?
Who would forget the relatives here?

S.FORM: supaR qenaswanita.
S.TRANSL: 我們應該記得我們全部的親友。
We should remember all our relatives.

S.FORM: ‘nay baqita yau tanian.
S.TRANSL: 那些長輩在哪裡?
Where are the elders?

S.FORM: supaRanta ti nani wanay baqian ti zuma Rayngu smaqay iku a sukaw sukaw ti izipku zinna nani.
S.TRANSL: 我老了不能走路了,我身體不好,他們說。
‘I am old (so) I cannot walk. My body is not good.’ said they.

S.FORM: mai ti ita mzukat tu zata ay qena qenalisibuan zinku.
S.TRANSL: 我們不參加我們的聚會。
We cannot participate in our gathering.

S.FORM: nayau ti azu nayau a yau a tanian saiza yau a mkikung ay.
S.TRANSL: 如果哪邊有婚禮，
If there is a wedding,

S.FORM: qawman wi ita pangmu e miza u qelawkaway saiza mai tu lazat qaniyau.
S.TRANSL: 我們一定去幫忙工作,(如果)他們人手不足。
We will definitely go and help (if) they do not have enough people.

S.FORM: qawman pangmuanta.
S.TRANSL: 我們一定要幫忙。
We must help (them).

S.FORM: azu kikung ay saiza anu mpa’tay ay qawman qatiwanta atu sunis sunis zinku.
S.TRANSL: 像結婚和有喪事的我們帶著孩子去, 我說。
If there is wedding or funeral, we will go there with our children, said I.

S.FORM: en nayau ti nayau ita sisunis tu Runanay nayauanku sunisku.
S.TRANSL: 我們有生男生的要這麼做。
(Those who) have boys must do this.

S.FORM: tiaki aki nayau Raya ti isu haw e mwaza ti tasawsu.
S.TRANSL: 我跟(我的孩子)Aki這麼說:Aki你長大了, 年紀大了。
I told (my child) Aki this: Aki, you’ve grown up.

S.FORM: naquni si lawlawutka tu qenaswani qaya zinku.
S.TRANSL: 如果這樣的話,你要常常拜訪親友, 我說。
If so, you should visit relatives often, said I.

S.FORM: nayau ti abu zinna.
S.TRANSL: 媽媽那樣說。
Mother said so.

S.FORM: wanayna ni aki wiya ti smani shangpan iku zinna.
S.TRANSL: 不知道去哪裡,他說:我去上班了。
(I) don’t know where he went. ‘I’m going to work,’ said he.

S.FORM: Ranaw naRin naRin sanpan zinku qawtuka ipil tu kunkuniq zinku nani.
S.TRANSL: 你先不要上班,來聽我們的故事,我說。
Don’t go to work. Come and listen to our stories, said I.

S.FORM: shangpan pa iku zinna.
S.TRANSL: 我要去上班,他說。
I’m going to work, said he.

S.FORM: yau ‘nay qelawkawayanna naquniakita.
S.TRANSL: 他有工作,我們沒辦法。
He has work. There is nothing we can do.

S.FORM: anu palezansu si.
S.TRANSL: 如果你把他留下來。
If you have him stay,

S.FORM: yau qelawkawayanku RaRiwanku si tinu ti mai zinna na s ya sunis kasianem.
S.TRANSL: 我有工作,如果我走了,誰要幫我做?他不會這樣想嗎?
I have work. If I leave, who will do the job? Wouldn’t he think so?

S.FORM: m nayau ti.
S.TRANSL: 也對啦！
That’s right.

S.FORM: maqen ti nikasianemansu ‘nay zana kwa zana niqalisinbuanta tazian tengnes ti ita.
S.TRANSL: 我們在這裡(指北區)的聚會已經辦很久了。
Our gathering here has existed for a long time.

S.FORM: Rabetin yau ulima pa ti haw.
S.TRANSL: Almost 15 (years).

S.FORM: en hoN Rabetin yau ulima pa ti tasaw niqalisinbuan tazian.
S.TRANSL: 我們在這裡的聚會已經快十五年了。
Our gathering here has lasted for almost fifteen years.

S.FORM: mana paqasengat tu zau qalisinbuan.
S.TRANSL: 我們為什麼要成立這個同鄉會?
Why did we set up this gathering?

S.FORM: supaRansu kwa.
S.TRANSL: 你也知道啊!
You know that.

S.FORM: yau ‘nay lanasta supaR pa ita sinsasanu.
S.TRANSL: 如果有事情我們才會聊聊談談。
If something happens, we can have a chat.

S.FORM: haN azu yau sapangmuan si qatiwanta mangmu qenaswanita zinna na.
S.TRANSL: 如果有要幫忙的人,我們會去幫忙。
If there are those who need help, we will go help our relatives.

S.FORM: nipaqalisinbuan tu zana zana muna ay nikasianeman tu zau.
S.TRANSL: 我們成立同鄉會是為了這樣的想法。
The gathering is for this kind of idea.

S.FORM: tu saqalisinbuan.
S.TRANSL: 我們要聚在一起的活動。
The activity whereby we would gather together.

S.FORM: nayau ti nayauakita wawayta saiza.
S.TRANSL: 就是這樣,我們就是要這樣做。
That’s it. We have to do so.

S.FORM: mai ti ita simqaRayngu nengi ti.
S.TRANSL: 我們不會忘記對方,就可以。
We won't forget one another. That's okay.

S.FORM: nazau wiyata matatanaq.
S.TRANSL: 分散的很遠。
We are separated far away (from each other).

S.FORM: yau tataipakan yau ta banqiaoan yau taxinzhuangan haw.
S.TRANSL: 有在台北的,有在板橋的,有在新莊的。
Some live in Taipei; some in Banqiao; some in Xinzhuang.

S.FORM: yau tataipakan yau ta banqiaoan yau taxinzhuangan haw.
S.TRANSL: 有在台北的,有在板橋的,有在新莊的。
Some live in Taipei; some in Banqiao; some in Xinzhuang.

S.FORM: mlawut azu aimi baibulan mai imi tu qaitun nani.
S.TRANSL: 像我們老人沒有車子。
We elders do not have cars.

S.FORM: yau imi talepawan atu tu sunis.
S.TRANSL: 我們跟小孩在家。
We stay at home with children.

S.FORM: yau qaitun numi si qatiwka lalawut mai pa mkalingu timaita tu.
S.TRANSL: 你們有車子的要常去拜訪,(他們)才不會忘記我們。
You have cars and should visit (others) often so that (they) would not forget us.

S.FORM: iza awka sunis mai pa wa mkalingu sikawmaan na kebalan saiza.
S.TRANSL: 小孩子才不會忘記噶瑪蘭話。
Children would not forget the language of Kavalan.

S.FORM: yau ti ya qawman mlizaq qaniyau.
S.TRANSL: 我們去的話,他們一定很高興。
If we go there, they would definitely be happy.

S.FORM: tudika ya sunisnumi zinta smanu.
S.TRANSL: 我們說:教你們的小孩子。
We would say, ‘Teach your children.’

S.FORM: si qasupaR ti sunisna anu tudanna.
S.TRANSL: 教的話,小孩子才會知道(我們的語言)。
If they teach the children, they will know (the language).

S.FORM: mai sanuanta mai imi sikawmaan jiao tu zana kebalan.
S.TRANSL: 我們沒有說他們沒有教噶瑪蘭。
We didn’t say that they did not teach Kavalan.

S.FORM: qaqbuRu ti ita tu kebalan naRin nayauan.
S.TRANSL: 我們會失去(我們的語言)噶瑪蘭,我們不能這樣。
We would lose Kavalan (our language). We cannot do so.

S.FORM: maqen ti maqen ti sikawmaan zau.
S.TRANSL: 你這樣說沒錯
What you said is right.

S.FORM: amawaR si qayau pama.
S.TRANSL: (噶瑪蘭語)以後還會有嗎?
Is it (the Kavalan language) going to survive in the future?

S.FORM: azu azu ti nisikawmaansu qaRpunguz ti sikawmaanta si.
S.TRANSL: 就像你所說的, 我們的語言可能就消失了。
Like what you’ve said, our language might disappear.

S.FORM: mai ti ita tu sikawmaan si qunian ti.
S.TRANSL: 我們沒有語言的話, 那該怎麼辦?
If we should lose our language, what could we do?

S.FORM: wantima baqian qawman.
S.TRANSL: 只有老人家才會教(噶瑪蘭語)。
Only the old can teach (the Kavalan language).

S.FORM: baqian ti ‘nay sunis mai qmulikuz tu sikawmaanta si.
S.TRANSL: 小孩子長大後, 沒有追隨著我們的語言的話。
If (our) children grow up without following our own language,

S.FORM: qayau pama sikawmaanta.
S.TRANSL: 我們的語言還會存留下來嗎?
Will our language still survive?

S.FORM: maqetun ti tayan.
S.TRANSL: (我們的語言)就斷在那裡了。
(Our language) may be lost right there.

S.FORM: ti aimu baibudan ti o ‘nay baqian si tud imi zinna na sunis sayzi nani.
S.TRANSL: 小孩子們也許會叫爺爺奶奶教他們(噶瑪蘭)。
Children may say “Grandpas, grandmas, teach us (Kavalan).”

S.FORM: wanay sunista mwaz mwaza ‘nay sataqsianna qaniyau tangi.
S.TRANSL: 因為我們的小孩子現在功課太多了。
(Because) now our children have so much to learn.

S.FORM: titaanta ‘nay izaanna kwa ‘nay mikangna tu pupuniR.
S.TRANSL: 我們也都看到了啊, 他們的書包都滿滿的。
We’ve seen, alas, their extremely full back-bags.

S.FORM: naqunianna pa ti smani sem smepez tu tauRu.
S.TRANSL: 到他們的腦袋裡面。
Into their heads.

S.FORM: tauRuanna tu sudad ‘nay zinta.
S.TRANSL: 我們就在想他們如何能把那麼多書裝進腦袋裡去。
We wonder how they manage to stuff all these books into their heads.

S.FORM: azu tangi yau tangi zuma si ngid patud tu patud tu sikawmaanta ya.
S.TRANSL: 今天還有其他的(問題), 比方說, 要教我們的語言的話。
Today there are other (problems), like, if (we) want to teach our language,

S.FORM: tu zana kebalan.
S.TRANSL: (教)噶瑪蘭的東西。
(teach) something about Kavalan

S.FORM: zana kebalan nani.
S.TRANSL: (教)噶瑪蘭的東西。
(teach) something about Kavalan

S.FORM: nengi ti.
S.TRANSL: 那好啊。
That’s good.

S.FORM: azu yau sakulikuzan si tu zana.
S.TRANSL: 比方說, 有可以跟隨(學習)的人的話 。
If there is someone (for them) to follow,

S.FORM: kebalan ‘nay mai pa mRapunguz.
S.TRANSL: 噶瑪蘭(的語言)才不會消失。
The Kavalan (language) will not disappear.

S.FORM: taqsianna pa na baqbaqianta sunsunista.
S.TRANSL: 我們的子子孫孫就能學習(噶瑪蘭的語言)。
Our posterity will learn (the Kavalan language).

S.FORM: qayau pa ti ya iza pa mai pa mai ya sikawmaanta zaka isu.
S.TRANSL: (我們的語言)才會存留下來, 那個, 我們的語言才不會沒有(i.e.消失),我這樣跟你說。
(Our language) may survive, and our language may not disappear. I told you so.

S.FORM: en nayaw ti.
S.TRANSL: 是那樣沒錯。
That’s right.

S.FORM: azu tangi tudan tu zana Rulam saiza sikawmaan qawman nayau ti.
S.TRANSL: 是啊, 現在教國語的語言也是那樣。
Yes, now the teaching of Mandarin is in the same situation.

S.FORM: kunkuna qaniyau pazukatanna taqsian na sunis.
S.TRANSL: 他們把他們的故事拿出來讓小孩學。
They took out their stories for children to learn.

S.FORM: ma sukaw msukaw Raqiya nayauanta pazuqat zana kebalan ay.
S.TRANSL: 我們就用類似的方法把噶瑪蘭的(故事)拿出來(教小孩), 難道不行嗎?
Can’t we also use Kavalan (stories to teach our children) in a similar manner? Won’t it work as well?

S.FORM: sikawmaan saiza niana padames pa ita sikawma tu bu baqbaqita.
S.TRANSL: 無論如何, 我們都要認真教小孩子。
No matter what may happen, we should work hard to teach our kids.

S.FORM: qayau pa sa sataqsianna sudadanna pa si.
S.TRANSL: (這樣才)可能有可以學習的東西和可以用來紀錄的東西。
(Only in this way) are there going to be what to learn and what to write with.

S.FORM: yau ti sa sa sapatudanna tu kaputna tu.
S.TRANSL: 他們要教全部的朋友的話。
In case they’d like to teach their friends.

S.FORM: qasupeR pa kaputna mniz si.
S.TRANSL: 他們的朋友才會懂。
Their friends can understand.

S.FORM: qasupeR pa tu zana kebalan zinku.
S.TRANSL: 才會懂噶瑪蘭的語言, 我想。
They would possibly undertand the Kavlan (language), I think.

S.FORM: azu qazui tagni ai mawtu aiku .
S.TRANSL: 如果以後他們說: 我們來過(ngengi這裡)。
If in the future they say, ‘We came here (ngengi’s place) once,’

S.FORM: pataqsian zau qawman nayau ti mtud tu sikawmaanta.
S.TRANSL: 也會那樣子教我們的語言。
(they) will also teach our language in that way.

S.FORM: nayau aka isu buya.
S.TRANSL: 我這樣跟你講,Buay。
I told you so, Buya.

S.FORM: nayau aka isu buya.
S.TRANSL: 我這樣跟你講,Buay。
I told you so, Buya.

S.FORM: nayau aka isu buya.
S.TRANSL: 我這樣跟你講,Buay。
I told you so, Buya.

S.FORM: nayau aka isu buya.
S.TRANSL: 我這樣跟你講,Buay。
I told you so, Buya.

S.FORM: nayau aka isu buya.
S.TRANSL: 我這樣跟你講,Buay。
I told you so, Buya.

S.FORM: qanengiaykati aisu sikawma tu patud tu pataqsianna.
S.TRANSL: 你要好好地講 ... 教學生。
You have to speak well ... teach the students.

S.FORM: kaput na qanizau na kinawsa ay zaka isu.
S.TRANSL: 你要好好教這兩位全部的朋友,我這樣跟你講。
You have to do your best to teach all the friends of these two (students), I told you so.

S.FORM: nengi ti nayau ‘nay sikawmaanta.
S.TRANSL: 我們這樣講沒錯。
What we said is right.

S.FORM: m yau anemku tu paqasengatan tu zana patudan tu sunista tazian.
S.TRANSL: 我有一個想法,成立教我們孩子的課程。
I have an idea to set up courses to teach our children here.

S.FORM: m wanay tababaw ay ngid tu zata tu naquniannumi pa kawka nengi si.
S.TRANSL: 上級要我們怎麼做才會好。
The superior wants us to ... What should we do?

S.FORM: pazukatika tu ni kasianemannumi kawka nengi.
S.TRANSL: 把你們的構想講出來才可以。
It’s good if you express your ideas.

S.FORM: supaR pa imi kasianem tu naqunianta pazukat tu kelisiw tu sa sangi saw sataqsian na sunis tazian zinna.
S.TRANSL: 我們會想辦法怎麼補助經費給小孩的教育用,他們說。
We will think about how to finance children’s education, said they.

S.FORM: oh nayau.
S.TRANSL: 嗯。
Oh.

S.FORM: yau anem saizi yau ‘nay ni sudadanku nani.
S.TRANSL: 是有心,我有寫(計劃)。
I am willing to..., and I have drafted (a project).

S.FORM: mai pama maseq qaya tu qanengian saiza nani.
S.TRANSL: 還沒完整。
It is not yet complete.

S.FORM: mai pama mzukat ti ya nani.
S.TRANSL: 還沒出來。
It is not finished yet.

S.FORM: palanaykati pasi pasi amawaR zuma ay tasaw si.
S.TRANSL: 再等一下,或許明年。
Later, maybe next year.

S.FORM: qazukat ti tu nazau ay sapatudanta tu sunista.
S.TRANSL: 我們要教小孩的(課程)會出來。
(The courses) we will teach our children will be completed.

S.FORM: u aita ba baqian nengi baibudan nengi ngid pama iku uman tu qasupeRanna qasupeRan tu zana s kebalan ‘nay sikawmaan si.
S.TRANSL: 我們老人家還要再更了解自己應該懂的噶瑪蘭話。
We elders still want to learn and understand more about the language of Kavalan again, which we should understand.

S.FORM: ipil pa iku uman tayan.
S.TRANSL: 我還要再去那裡聽。
I will (attend classes) there again.

S.FORM: taqsi pa iku uman tayan.
S.TRANSL: 我還要再去那裡學。
I will study there again.

S.FORM: mai zinna qaya na baqian kasianem.
S.TRANSL: 老人們不是這樣想嗎?
Don’t the elders think so?

S.FORM: mai imi mai iku tu wama sunis zin na anem.
S.TRANSL: 我不是只針對小孩。
I’m not referring to children only.

S.FORM: baqian baibudan yau yau anemna ngid iza sinananem sinananem tayan si.
S.TRANSL: 如果(老人家)他們有心想到那邊學習。
If (the elders) they want to study there,

S.FORM: nizanta mala zinku ya.
S.TRANSL: 我們全都接收。
We will accept all of them.

S.FORM: nayau ti azu zuma matiw ita salekiaw saiza satzay.
S.TRANSL: 的確是那樣. 比方說我們有時候去唱歌跳舞的時候。
That’s it. For example when we sometimes go dancing and singing,

S.FORM: u yau ti kebalan en zinna.
S.TRANSL: 他們就會說「喔, 噶瑪蘭人來了」。
They’d say ‘Oh, here come the Kavalan people.’

S.FORM: quniaka tmud.
S.TRANSL: 我怎麼教呢?
How can I teach?

S.FORM: azu yau taqsian nani tud zinku nengi ti satuRin anemku tmud timaimu zinku.
S.TRANSL: 如果有可以學的地方的話, 我才好教. 我說「我很願意教你們」。
Only if there are places for learning would it be convenient for me to teach. ‘I’m willing to teach you,’ I said.’

S.FORM: u quniakita haw tud imi tu utulu zinna.
S.TRANSL: 他們說「喔, 我們該怎麼辦呢? 教我們兩三句吧」。
They said, ‘Oh, what should we do? Teach us two or three (sentences).’

S.FORM: azu ti siRab qataban tayan titaika ya.
S.TRANSL: 比方說, 你看看前陣子的Qataban豐年祭。
For instance, take a look at the Qataban festival that took place the other day.

S.FORM: yau ti imi tayan ti tidungi kwa ta ‘nay masusulun timaimuan nani.
S.TRANSL: 我們在那裡跟dungi一起, 就是你們常跟他在一起的那個dungi。
We were there with dungi, whom you often get together with.

S.FORM: a akwa satzayka zinna tu zana kebalan yau ti imi kaysa satzay zana kebalan.
S.TRANSL: 他們說「快點來唱噶瑪蘭的歌!」於是我們就開始唱噶瑪蘭的歌了。
They said, ‘Hurry up! Sing the Kavalan songs.’ So we started to sing the Kavalan songs.

S.FORM: tu mRayngu mana nayau imu ya a kyunumi Rayngu sikwama.
S.TRANSL: 不知道為什麼你們的發音會那樣,你們都不會說。
I don’t know why you guys’s pronunciation turned out to be like this. You guys cannot speak.

S.FORM: mai imi nayau kebalan ya.
S.TRANSL: 我們噶瑪蘭人才不會那樣。
We Kavalan are not like that.

S.FORM: man usiqkisay msikawma nani supeR ti imi.
S.TRANSL: 只要說了一次, 我們就懂了。
As long as it’s spoken once, we’d understand it.

S.FORM: man man zipun man Rulam man kizaya man niana supeR imu sikawmaan zinna.
S.TRANSL: 不管是說日語,國語,阿美語,還是任何語言其他語言. 他們說「你們(噶瑪蘭)都會說」。’
(Whether it is) Japanese, Mandarin, Amis, or any other languges. They said, ‘you (Kavalan) are good at speaking languages.’

S.FORM: u maqen satuRin imu kebalan zinna iku.
S.TRANSL: 他們跟我說 「哇, 你們噶瑪蘭人真的很厲害捏。」
They said to me, ‘Wow, you Kavalan people are really skillful.’

S.FORM: yau ti tudanku nianu qaya mRaRiw ya ya ya li sikawmaan na lilamna tu nayau taqsi pa imi tu zana kebalan hada zinna.
S.TRANSL: 他們說「我們打算要學噶瑪蘭語」。
They said, ‘We are going to learn the Kavalan language.’

S.FORM: kwa ya qatiw pa imi taqsi zinna iku .
S.TRANSL: 他們跟我說「好啊, 我們要去學」。
They said to me, ‘Okay, we’re going to learn.’

S.FORM: nengi ti ya aisu patud ay nengi ti.
S.TRANSL: 你來教的話,很好啊。
It’s good that you’re the one who teaches.

S.FORM: qawman yau patud ay qawman mlizaq qaniyau ‘nay.
S.TRANSL: 有人教就很高興了。
It’s good that there’s someone who can teach. I am quite happy about it.

S.FORM: nayau qaniyau mazmun iza tud imi zinna.
S.TRANSL: 因此, 他們很多人就說「教我們啊」。
So many of them said, ‘Teach us.’

S.FORM: naquni nisikawmaannumi Rayngu imi mipil zinna.
S.TRANSL: 他們說「怎麼我們都聽不懂你們說的話」。
They said, ‘How come we cannot understand what you said?’

S.FORM: u kaya satuRin ita kebalan zinku yu.
S.TRANSL: 這樣說來, 我想我們噶瑪蘭人是很厲害的。
I think we Kavalan people are quite competent.

S.FORM: maqen ti supaR tu en zau smalad tu ninu tu sikawmaan na ‘daq.
S.TRANSL: 真的是這樣, (我們)能夠回應別人說的任何語言。
That’s true. (We) can respond to any languages of others.

S.FORM: nayau mai ita qaqimannungan kebalan .
S.TRANSL: 我們噶瑪蘭人不會被人家批評。
We Kavalan people will not be criticized.

S.FORM: maqen mai ita qaqimannungan sayzi.
S.TRANSL: 沒錯, 就不會被批評了。
Indeed, we may not be criticized.

S.FORM: m nayau a wawayta na kebalan satuRin.
S.TRANSL: 我們噶瑪蘭人的舉止就是像那樣, 非常能幹。
So that’s the way we Kavalan behave. We’re very competent.

S.FORM: qatuRiyas mala mai azu qaniyau tu zau zinta nani o maspalaki mana smani.
S.TRANSL: 馬上就能學會, 不像他們不知道為什麼舌頭老是會打結。
We can acquire it at once. We think we are not like them. I don’t know why their tongues always knot up.

S.FORM: mabaliunes qaya sikawmaan na qaniyau nani naquniakita.
S.TRANSL: 他們講的話還會歪七扭八. 我們該怎麼才好呢?’
Their speech also gets twisted. What shall we do about it?

S.FORM: ‘nay zana qaniyau ay iza sikawmaan supaR qaniyau nani supaR qaniyau sikawaanna ya aita nani supaR aita mipil saiza qa saizi ‘nay tu zana qaniyau ay nani.
S.TRANSL: 他們會的話(Amis),我們也會聽他們的話(Amis)。
They know the language (Amis). We understand their language (Amis) as well.

S.FORM: wanay ipilan aita sikawma tu zata ay sikawmaanta kebalan nani.
S.TRANSL: 他聽到我們講自己的噶瑪蘭話時。
When they hear us speaking our language, Kavalan,

S.FORM: mai mana nayau ka azu ti ‘nay qimanung zinna.
S.TRANSL: 他們怎麼會認為我們說他們的壞話?
How come they would think that we were speaking ill of them?

S.FORM: mai qimanung saizi nani.
S.TRANSL: 我們絕對沒有說他們的壞話。
We were definitely not speaking ill of them.

S.FORM: qimanung imu timaimi zinna.
S.TRANSL: 他們說:你們講我們的壞話。
‘You are speaking ill of us,’ said they.

S.FORM: qaniyau Rayngu mipil ma.
S.TRANSL: 只是他們不會聽而已。
They don’t understand (Kavalan).

S.FORM: e heN qimanung imu zinna mai imi qimanung.
S.TRANSL: 他們說 ‘你們講壞話,’(可是),我們沒有講壞話。
They said, ‘You were speaking ill of (us),’ but we were not speaking ill (of them.)

S.FORM: sikaw pakunku imi tiamuy ma matabung imi tiamuy nani.
S.TRANSL: 我們和Amuy碰面。
We met Amuy.

S.FORM: tmangkebalan imi tiamuy.
S.TRANSL: 我們講噶瑪蘭。
We spoke Kavalan.

S.FORM: u niana zinnumi ti kinawsa.
S.TRANSL: 你們兩個在講什麼?
What were you two talking about?

S.FORM: mana wa a estaR ti imi tu izau Rayngu ti imi mipil zinna.
S.TRANSL: 為什麼我們好像,我們不會聽, 他們這麼說。
How come it seems that we could not understand (Kavalan), said they.

S.FORM: anu matabung ita nani tang kizaya aimu ka.
S.TRANSL: 我們在一起時,你們都講Amis。
When we met, you spoke Amis.

S.FORM: a supaR aimi tu kizaya qu mana nayau imi Rayngu imi zinna tu.
S.TRANSL: 他們說:我們也知道Amis, 我們怎麼會這樣子, 我們都不會(噶瑪蘭) 。
‘We know Amis. How come we are like this. We do not understand (Kavalan),’ said they.

S.FORM: nawsiRap qataban aimi.
S.TRANSL: 前陣子我們豐年祭。
Some time ago, we had the harvest festival ‘qataban.’

S.FORM: qaRabi nayau qaya matiw imi tawian takongyuanan.
S.TRANSL: 昨天晚上,我們去公園,也是那樣。
Last night, we went to the park, and they did so again.

S.FORM: o amuy e RumeRika ya kapin ‘nay zinku nani.
S.TRANSL: 我說: amuy,把紫菜醃一醃。
‘Amuy, pickcle the laver,’ said I.

S.FORM: aw niana zinnumi ti qimanung zinna.
S.TRANSL: 他們(Amis)說:你們說了什麼壞話。
‘What were you critizing for?,’ said they (Amis).

S.FORM: usa iku qimanung.
S.TRANSL: 我們不是說壞話。
We did not speak ill (of somebody.

S.FORM: tang tang kizayaanku ti RumeRika ya ya ‘nay kapin ‘nay ya kepuli.
S.TRANSL: 醃紫菜和綠海菜。
Pickle the laver and green seaweed.

S.FORM: yau a sili yau a mian zinku tu.
S.TRANSL: 我說,有辣椒,有鹽。
‘There is pepper and there is salt,’ said I.

S.FORM: RumeRanna ti amuy .
S.TRANSL: Amui就醃(紫菜和綠海菜) 。
Amuy pickled (the laver and green seaweed).

S.FORM: tiana mala ay zinna.
S.TRANSL: 他們說,是誰拿的?
‘Who took them?’ said they.

S.FORM: aiku ya tianu mala ay qaya.
S.TRANSL: (Amui說,)是我,不然是誰拿的。
(Amui said,) ‘It’s I, or who would take them?’

S.FORM: sunisku matiw qikapin zinku.
S.TRANSL: 我說: 我的小孩去撿紫菜。
My child went to pick laver, said I.

S.FORM: satuRin sunissu haw.
S.TRANSL: 你的小孩很厲害。
You child is competent.

S.FORM: qikapin nayau saizi aimi na kebalan wama lazing zinniq.
S.TRANSL: 我們噶瑪蘭只有海邊。
‘We Kavalan only have the sea,’ said we.

S.FORM: matiw matiw imi sa lazing nani.
S.TRANSL: 我們去海邊。
We go to the sea.

S.FORM: wiya ti qikapin saiza qi qiqaiR saiza qi qabus ya Runanay.
S.TRANSL: 男生可能去撿紫菜九孔和龍蝦。
Males perhaps go pick laver, Taiwanese abalone, and lobsters.

S.FORM: aimi tazungan wiya ti imi qibuqut saiza qibais imi nani.
S.TRANSL: 我們女生,我們可能就去撿石鱉和笠螺。
(As for) we females, we perhaps go pick chitons and limpets.

S.FORM: Ramazanniq ti talazing.
S.TRANSL: 就在海邊煮了。
We cooked on the seaside.

S.FORM: aita kebalan haw temaqanta.
S.TRANSL: 我們噶瑪蘭,我們用火烤。
We Kavalan, we roast (them).

S.FORM: azu swaniku tiawun ya manani e manan ita u matiw ita sa lazing nani.
S.TRANSL: 像我的妹妹Awun,我們從海邊回來的時候。
Like my sister, Awun, when we came back from the sea,

S.FORM: pa mala ti tu tamun tu niquayanta patapunanna ti qibasianna ti nani.
S.TRANSL: 我們拿菜,我們撿的東西,使集中,清洗。
We took vegetable and what we had picked. We gathered and washed them.

S.FORM: pun ti pizyaanna ti tapaduan.
S.TRANSL: 放在檳榔葉做的容器裡。
(We) put them in a container made of the leaves of betel nut trees.

S.FORM: temaq ti tu betu pun temaq tu betu nani.
S.TRANSL: 燒完石頭之後。
After burning stones,

S.FORM: padiqebunanna ti ya ya betu ‘nay sabiqbiq ti tu mana nengi qanan saiza niRamazan.
S.TRANSL: 滾燙後,很好吃。
After becoming boiling hot, what we cooked were delicious.

S.FORM: tu nazauanta mai lasam.
S.TRANSL: 我們這樣做,不會髒。
We did in this way, and would not make the pot dirty.

S.FORM: azu temeqanta tengeng siwi.
S.TRANSL: 如果我們用煮的,鍋子會變黑。
If we cooked, the pot would become black.

S.FORM: nazau en.
S.TRANSL: 對。
Yes.

S.FORM: e yau ‘nay iza ‘nay temaqan nengi pama temaqansu nani.
S.TRANSL: 如果有(可以)烤的(東西), 你也可以用烤的。
If there is something that can be roasted, you can also roast it.

S.FORM: bautta temaqanta tu mlitungtung izau bangsis.
S.TRANSL: 我們把魚烤到很香。
We roast fish to the extent that they smell good.

S.FORM: en.
S.TRANSL: 對.
Yes.

S.FORM: e bangsis ti baRi ti ‘nay salangna nani.
S.TRANSL: 魚很香了以後, 魚湯也就紅了。
When they smell good, the fish soup also turns red.

S.FORM: naRin m naRin naRin izaan tu pilidta m mRamaz.
S.TRANSL: 不要用鍋子煮。
Don’t cook with the wok.

S.FORM: pangmenganta sabengneq nengi ti.
S.TRANSL: 我們只要用Sabengneq的方式料理魚就可以了。
We can still (cook fish) simply in a manner of “sabengneq” instead.

S.FORM: nayau ti.
S.TRANSL: 的確是那樣。
Indeed, that’s the way it is.

S.FORM: sabengneqan qawka nengi maqen ‘nay zana kebalan tu si tu nitemaqan ‘nay baut tu.
S.TRANSL: 只有Sabengneq才是真正的噶瑪蘭的烤魚方式。
Only our “Sabengneq” is the real Kavalan way of roasting fish.

S.FORM: nisyanguzusan nizan nikuwayanta talazingan.
S.TRANSL: 我們在海邊的收穫全部都是在海邊撿東西。
All that we get is collected along the coastline.

S.FORM: nayau ti.
S.TRANSL: 的確是那樣。
Indeed, that’s the way it is.

S.FORM: a matiw sa lazing aita zata kebalan ya.
S.TRANSL: 我們噶瑪蘭去海邊的時候。
(When) we Kavalan go to the sea,

S.FORM: anu matiw ita sa iRuR nani wiya ti.
S.TRANSL: 像去河邊。
For example, we go to the riverside.

S.FORM: azu tamaku ya.
S.TRANSL: 比方說, 我爸爸。
For example, my father.

S.FORM: wi ni saiRuR pa ita zinna nani.
S.TRANSL: 我爸爸說「走吧, 我們去河邊」。
My father said, ‘Let’s go. We’re going to the river.’

S.FORM: temawan ti tu tataq sayza pungki sayza ‘nay bubuna.
S.TRANSL: 就準備了鋤頭, 畚箕, 還有他的bubu。
He prepared the hoe, the dustpan maybe, and his ‘bubu’.

S.FORM: wiya ti imi sa iRuR nani.
S.TRANSL: 於是我們就去了河邊。
There we went to the river.

S.FORM: tabalanna ti iRuR ‘nay.
S.TRANSL: 是要去擋住河流了吧。
He was going to block the river, wasn’t he?

S.FORM: patap tapu pa ita zinna.
S.TRANSL: 他(我爸爸)說, 「我們要堵住...」
He (my father) said, ‘We’re going to obsrtruct...’

S.FORM: matiw ti imi tapuanniq ti Raya ay iRuR.
S.TRANSL: 我們就去把那條大河流堵住。
We went to obstruct the great river.

S.FORM: pun ti nani pakelanganniq ti ya u kapusna sayza kumuna sayza waRangna.
S.TRANSL: 把河流堵好了以後, 我們就到處翻找蝦子啦, Kumu魚啦, 或是螃蟹等等。
After it was done, we looked here and there (on the bed of the river) for shrimps, ‘kumu’, or crabs.

S.FORM: niz ti nialaanniq pun ti nani.
S.TRANSL: 我們把全部都撿完以後。
After we finished picking everything,

S.FORM: pabubuanna ti talibeng.
S.TRANSL: 就把Bubu放在下面。
He put the Bubu downstream.

S.FORM: maytis paRaRiwanna sayza na masalin ay qabusna bautna nani.
S.TRANSL: 因為怕漂流的魚蝦會跑走。
Being afraid that the floating shrimps and fish might run away,

S.FORM: s pasababawanna ti sangkuna.
S.TRANSL: 於是就把Sangku(的開口)面向上游。
He made the Sangku face upstream.

S.FORM: qman qan pa ita ti satutu zinna nani.
S.TRANSL: 「吃...我們去吃午餐吧」他說。
‘Eat.. let’s have lunch,’ he said.

S.FORM: yau ti imi mRamaz sayza qman ti imi nani.
S.TRANSL: 可能我們還在煮飯,或是已經吃完的時候。
Perhaps when we were cooking or when we had finished our lunch,

S.FORM: yau yau bubu ‘nay smaRuR qa qabus sayza nani.
S.TRANSL: Bubu還放在河裡. 當蝦子往下游的時候。
The Bubu was still there. When the shrimps went downstream,

S.FORM: taRaanna ti na nengi ti.
S.TRANSL: 很好。
That’s good.

S.FORM: izaan na bubu ‘nay.
S.TRANSL: (蝦子)就會被Bubu盛接住。
(The shrimps) would be caught in the Bubu.

S.FORM: mai ti ita pakelang nayau.
S.TRANSL: 這樣我們就不用到處翻石頭找魚蝦了。
So we don’t have to look for them.

S.FORM: niz ti ta sangkuanna na smusuR tasangkuan.
S.TRANSL: 全部的(蝦子)都會進到sangku。
All (shrimps) would enter the Sangku.

S.FORM: en wantima sayza waRang mai smaRuR nani.
S.TRANSL: 是啊, 可能只有螃蟹不會往下游走。
Yes, maybe only crabs would not go downstream.

S.FORM: ‘nay buquwatanniq ti betu nani.
S.TRANSL: 所以我們就把石頭翻開。
So we turned over the stones.

S.FORM: kyalaanniq ti ya waRang sayza mai smaRuR ay.
S.TRANSL: 我們就去撿那些不會往下游走的螃蟹。
We picked up the crabs that would not go downstream.

S.FORM: nayau a waway na kebalan.
S.TRANSL: 那樣就是噶瑪蘭人的習俗。
That’s Kavalan’s custom.

S.FORM: en maqen ti ‘nay iza tayan ta tadamu tadamuan masang ni izaanta.
S.TRANSL: 以前在村莊的時候,真的是這樣。
Indeed, that is the way it was in the village before.

S.FORM: ni ququnian pakelangan matiw sa lazing sa iRuRan nizu.
S.TRANSL: 不管是去找尋魚蝦啦, 還是去河邊, 去海邊啦, 全部都是做這些事情。
That’s all we did, such as looking for (shrimps, fish, crabs, etc.) and going to the seashore, to the river.

S.FORM: maqen ‘nay mai tu mRamaz tu ninu.
S.TRANSL: 真的是這樣, (我們也)沒煮什麼。
That’s true. (We) cook nothing special.

S.FORM: wama zana iRuR ay zana iRuR ay ma tamunta.
S.TRANSL: 只有河邊的東西的話, 我們的菜就是河邊的東西。
If we only have those from the river, those from the river will be our dishes.

S.FORM: zana lazing ay wama zana lazing ay tamunta.
S.TRANSL: 只有海邊的東西的話, 我們的菜就是海邊的東西。
If we only have those from the sea, those from the sea will be our dishes.

S.FORM: nayau maqen ‘nay zana qenabinus na kebalan tayan ta tadamuan masang zinku ya.
S.TRANSL: 我想那的確是從前噶蘭人還住在村莊裡時的生活方式。
I think that’s really the life Kavalan people lived in the village before.

S.FORM: azu masang haw yau yau pama iku ta damuanta tapateRungan nani.
S.TRANSL: 就像過去我們還在故鄉,在新社時。
Like in the past when we were still in our village, in Xinshe.

S.FORM: sianguzus pa ita zin na bai nani.
S.TRANSL: 阿媽說: 我們去海邊撿食物吧。
‘Let’s go pick up food at sea,’ said Grandmother.

S.FORM: yau ti imi sa izau qatiw pa ita sa lazing zinna.
S.TRANSL: 他們說: 我們去這個,我們去海邊。
Let’s go ... this ... Let’s go to the sea, said they

S.FORM: tu tmabu ti imi tu ‘may.
S.TRANSL: 我們包了飯。
We packed rice.

S.FORM: wama mian nani sili nitabuniq.
S.TRANSL: 包的東西只有鹽和辣椒。
The things we packed were only salt and pepper.

S.FORM: wiya ti imi sa lazing.
S.TRANSL: 我們到了海邊。
We went to the sea.

S.FORM: m yau ti imi sianguzus nani.
S.TRANSL: 我們就開始撿食物。
We began to pick up food.

S.FORM: m ‘nay yau a qa manan ti imi matiw sianguzus.
S.TRANSL: 我們去海邊撿食物回來了。
We returned after picking up food at the seashore.

S.FORM: mwaza ya nikuayanniq nani.
S.TRANSL: 我們採的東西很多。
We picked many things.

S.FORM: yau zuma mawtu yau labangta ya baqung saiza tanian ‘nay nani.
S.TRANSL: 另外,我們有客人來,從豐濱那裡。
In addition, we had guests from Fong-bin or elsewhere.

S.FORM: m mai siangu m mai Rayngu pama sianguzus qaniyau kya.
S.TRANSL: 他們還不會在海邊撿海菜
They still cannot pick up food at the seashore.

S.FORM: m wa supaR aimu ka sianguzus zinna tu.
S.TRANSL: 他們說: 哇!你們很會撿海邊的東西。
‘Wow! You knew how to pick up food at sea,’ said they.

S.FORM: pangmu amawaR matiw ti zinna sa lazing zinna kwa.
S.TRANSL: 他們說: 改天去海邊幫我們。
‘Someday go to the sea and help us,’ said they.

S.FORM: pun ti nani Rayngu mumas qaniyau bangtu.
S.TRANSL: 完成後,他們不會醃, 會臭掉。
After it’s done, they could not preserve the meat. (The food) would rot.

S.FORM: nayau qaniyau masang.
S.TRANSL: 他們以前就是那樣。
They were like that in the past.

S.FORM: wama aita kebalan ya supaR tu quayan tu iza saiza ta ya tu satamunan nani ya aita na kebalan.
S.TRANSL: 只有我們噶瑪蘭很會撿海邊的東西,要吃的菜。
Only we Kavalan are able to pick up food at sea ... the meals.

S.FORM: masang azu taika qani amuy masang Rayngu sianguzusan.
S.TRANSL: 看,Amuy他們以前不會撿海邊的食物。
Look, they did not know how to pick food at seaside in the past.

S.FORM: tinana sazna qaniyau mai mai tu dawad dawad.
S.TRANSL: 他們以前耕田,沒有時間。
They tilled the land in the past and did not have time.

S.FORM: mai tu dawad matiw sa lazing.
S.TRANSL: 沒有時間去海邊。
(They) did not have time to go to the sea.

S.FORM: tu mai tu patud ay tu qatiw sa lazing.
S.TRANSL: 又沒有人教他們去海邊(撿什麼) 。
There was no one who could teach them (what they should pick up when) going to the sea.

S.FORM: tu naqunianta ni sianguzusan.
S.TRANSL: 撿回來的東西怎麼處理?
How did we do with what we picked at sea?

S.FORM: m ‘nay iza tu nialaanna qaniyau Rayngu ti Rayngu mumas Rayngu mumas saiza nani .
S.TRANSL: 他們拿回來的東西,他們不會醃。
They might not be able to preserve what they had taken back.

S.FORM: babangtu ti pamenganna ti mRamaz ya.
S.TRANSL: 臭掉了,他們只好用煮的。
(The food) would rot, (so) they could only cook them.

S.FORM: qanibaibaita masang nani tu buqutna utulu bulan saiza qeni qenibulan nani tangananna ti.
S.TRANSL: 我們阿媽以前,他們的石鱉,(醃了)三個月後,打開。
Our grandmother in the past ... their chitons ... after three months’ preserving, they opened (the container).

S.FORM: mai ti tu ku mai ti tu buwang usa m sutubedun sutuiza ti esina na esi ti o .
S.TRANSL: 石鱉的殼就掉落了,只有剩下肉了。
There were no shells but only meat.

S.FORM: naquni niumasan na baibaita masang nayauanna.
S.TRANSL: 不管怎麼樣,我們阿媽以前醃的東西就是那樣做的。
In any event, the things our grandmother preserved were like that.

S.FORM: baibaita masang tangi azu sunista tangi supaR pama sianguzus Raynug ti.
S.TRANSL: 像我們現在的小孩還會去撿海邊的食物嗎? 不會。
Could our children now pick up food at seaside? No.

S.FORM: ngidanku tud e sunisnumi zinku yu.
S.TRANSL: 我想教你們的孩子。
I want to teach your children.

S.FORM: azas zaka sa lazing pasianguzus zaka.
S.TRANSL: 我想讓他們去撿海邊的食物。
I want to have them go to the sea and pick food there.

S.FORM: u matiw sa iRuR ka.
S.TRANSL: 或者到河邊。
or going to the river

S.FORM: papakelanganku tiaki zinku yu.
S.TRANSL: 我要讓Aki抓魚。
I’ll have Aki catch fish.

S.FORM: supaR Rayngu ti mai ti mai tmita tu makelang.
S.TRANSL: 他從未看過如何抓魚。
He has never seen how to catch fish.

S.FORM: qunianta makelang mai zinna.
S.TRANSL: 我們是怎麼抓魚的?他不會這樣想嗎?
How do we catch fish? Wouldn’t he think so?

S.FORM: qawman patudan qawman supaR ti.
S.TRANSL: 你教他,他一定會的。
(If) you teach him, he will certainly know it.

S.FORM: qawman amawaR si azu yau dawadsu si azasika mniz ‘nay sunis mniz si.
S.TRANSL: 下次如果你有空,就帶全部的小孩們。
Next time if you have time, take all the children ...

S.FORM: tud ya tudika ti tu naqunianta pakelang naqunianta sianguzus.
S.TRANSL: 你就教他們我們是如何抓魚,我們是如何在海邊撿食物的。
Teach them how we catch fish and how we pick up food at sea.

S.FORM: zika ya.
S.TRANSL: 你就這樣跟他們講。
Tell them thus.

S.FORM: nayau nayau ma sikawmaanta ya tangi o en mlaziw ti izata.
S.TRANSL: 今天我們講的就這樣,我們已經超過那個了(指時間) 。
That’s all of what we said today. Time is up.

S.FORM: en.
S.TRANSL: 好的。
Okay.
